records.warning: Candidate-only pilot. Not official EMS damage and not public operational evidence.
records.name: Residencias Basento
records.before_source: Vantor Open Data pre-event reference scene B160001100FD1910, 2026-03-20T14:46:55Z, not official EMS before imagery
records.after_source: Copernicus EMSR884 AOI03 post-event Legion COG, 2026-06-25T15:17:00Z

vlm.change_evidence: The post-event chip is almost entirely blank/white with no surface features visible—only the OSM footprint outline and centroid reticle are rendered. No building, ground texture, debris, or shadow information is present in the after image, so no before/after physical comparison can be made.
vlm.before_observation: Pre-event Vantor reference shows an intact multi-story apartment building (Residencias Basento) within the yellow footprint outline. Visible features include a defined roofline, structural mass, and shadow casting consistent with an undamaged mid-rise building. Surrounding urban context (adjacent buildings, road, median vegetation) is clearly resolved.
vlm.after_observation: Post-event Copernicus EMSR884 AOI03 chip is effectively empty: a uniform white/very pale background with no building geometry, no shadow, no debris field, and no ground texture inside or around the footprint. No features are discernible.
vlm.image_alignment: Cannot be evaluated. The after image contains no scene content, so co-registration or offset between scenes cannot be confirmed. Footprint outline position is consistent with before in pixel space, but the lack of after content prevents any geometric check.
vlm.image_quality: Before: good resolution, clear, low haze. After: unusable—appears to be missing data, a no-data fill, heavy cloud/opaque cover, or a failed render. The after tile does not contain surface reflectance information at the candidate location.
vlm.uncertainty_reason: The post-event chip provides no usable visual information at the candidate location. It is uniformly white with no terrain or building features. Triage is impossible: damage cannot be confirmed, and apparent 'disappearance' of the building cannot be distinguished from a missing/corrupted post-event tile, full cloud cover, or a sensor/data delivery issue. This candidate must be re-imaged or a replacement post-event source retrieved before any damage call is made.

records.after_source: Copernicus EMSR884 AOI03 post-event Legion COG, 2026-06-25T15:17:00Z

vlm.change_evidence: Within the yellow/black candidate footprint, the pre-event scene shows a clearly defined multi-story building with visible rooftop structure (mechanical/roof features) and cast shadows indicating substantial height. In the post-event scene, the same footprint contains bright white/light-toned material with irregular texture consistent with debris or exposed rubble; the original roofline and rooftop features are no longer discernible, and the height/shadow signature has been lost.
vlm.before_observation: The candidate footprint contains a distinct apartment block (Bloque 4) with a complex multi-level roof, visible rooftop equipment/structure, and clear vertical shadows indicating an occupied multi-story building. Surrounding context includes roads, vegetation, and adjacent built structures with normal urban appearance.
vlm.after_observation: Within the same footprint, the previous building form is replaced by a bright white/light-toned irregular pattern suggestive of rubble, debris, or a heavily collapsed structure. No clear roofline, rooftop features, or vertical shadow consistent with the prior building height are visible. Surrounding buildings and road network appear largely in place, indicating a localized change rather than a scene-wide event.
vlm.image_alignment: Roughly co-registered; the yellow footprint overlay sits in the same approximate location in both chips. Some scale/resolution mismatch is present but does not prevent footprint-level comparison.
vlm.image_quality: Before: good resolution, sharp. After: lower resolution, notable haze/atmospheric softness, slightly washed-out tones — still sufficient to see major structural change within the footprint but reduces confidence in fine detail.
vlm.uncertainty_reason: Resolution and haze in the post-event image limit certainty about whether the white material represents total collapse versus partial damage with debris accumulation or a temporary covering. Higher-resolution follow-up imagery is needed to confirm destruction grade.

vlm.change_evidence: Within the OSM footprint outline, the pre-event image shows a clearly defined building structure with a discernible roof. In the post-event image, the same footprint area shows a lighter-toned, less defined rectangular mass that appears consistent with a partially collapsed structure or debris field rather than an intact roofed building. The change is visible but the post-event scene's heavy haze/low contrast limits confidence.
vlm.before_observation: Building footprint visible with apparent roof structure, light-colored roof surface distinguishable from surrounding vegetation and ground. Adjacent buildings, trees, and a curving road to the west are clearly resolved.
vlm.after_observation: Same footprint shows a pale, roughly rectangular feature suggestive of collapsed/flattened structure or exposed floor/rubble. Surrounding vegetation, road, and neighboring buildings are not resolvable; the scene is dominated by uniform low-contrast haze or possible smoke/atmosphere effects.
vlm.image_alignment: Footprint outline location and orientation appear consistent between the two chips; both reticle/centroid positions align within the same candidate building area. Coarse scale agreement is acceptable.
vlm.image_quality: Before image: good resolution, full color, clear. After image: markedly degraded — low contrast, desaturated/grayscale appearance, hazy atmosphere, loss of contextual features (vegetation, roads, neighbors). Comparison is constrained by after-image quality.
vlm.uncertainty_reason: Post-event chip has poor contrast and apparent atmospheric haze/smoke, obscuring fine structural detail. The lighter patch within the footprint is consistent with collapse/debris but could also reflect a damaged but still-standing roof under poor imaging conditions. Insufficient resolution and contrast to confirm major damage versus partial/minor damage.

vlm.change_evidence: In the before chip, a clear multi-story apartment building with visible roof structure, walls, and defined footprint occupies the candidate outline. In the after chip, the same footprint area shows a darker, uniform, flattened surface with no discernible roof, walls, or height signature — consistent with collapse or severe structural loss. Surrounding context (vehicles, adjacent structures) is visible in both, but the after scene is heavily pixelated.
vlm.before_observation: Multi-story apartment block with light/gray pitched or flat roof, clearly defined edges, and visible structural details within the yellow OSM outline. Surrounding paved lot and adjacent buildings are sharp.
vlm.after_observation: Footprint area appears as a dark, flat, homogeneous patch without visible roofline, walls, or shadow of a standing structure. No clear debris pile texture, but loss of all height/roof signal. Adjacent features (parked vehicles, nearby buildings) still visible but rendered at much lower resolution with blocky pixelation.
vlm.image_alignment: Reasonable spatial co-registration; the yellow OSM footprint sits over the same geographic location in both chips. Slight rotation/scale differences, but candidate footprint is geographically consistent.
vlm.image_quality: Before: high-resolution, sharp, good contrast. After: low-resolution, heavy compression/pixelation, blocky artifacts. Sufficient to detect absence of building form, but insufficient to confidently characterize debris vs. flat shadow or low-relief ruin.
vlm.uncertainty_reason: After image is severely degraded in resolution and sharpness, so although the building's roof and walls are no longer visible, I cannot distinguish between complete destruction, partial collapse with rubble spread, or a low-relief remnant under poor imagery. Confidence is therefore limited.

vlm.before_observation: Clear aerial imagery showing a building with a visible orange/red roof within the yellow OSM footprint. Surrounding context includes vegetation, other buildings, a parking area with vehicles, and a road network. High resolution and good color/contrast.
vlm.after_observation: Very dark, low-contrast, low-resolution image. The OSM footprint is barely discernible against a near-black background. No clear roof structure, walls, or building features are visible within the footprint. Some bright white patches appear in the upper portion of the scene, outside the candidate area. The interior of the footprint is essentially uninterpretable due to image quality.
vlm.image_alignment: The OSM footprint outline and reticle appear to be positioned consistently between the two images; co-registration looks acceptable at the chip level.
vlm.image_quality: Before: high resolution, good color, clear features. After: very poor — dark, low contrast, low effective resolution, heavy compression, near-monochrome. The post-event image quality is insufficient for reliable visual comparison of structural condition within the candidate footprint.
vlm.uncertainty_reason: The after image is too dark and low-resolution to determine whether the building still stands, has collapsed, or is merely obscured by the imagery's poor quality. The dramatic difference in image quality between before and after scenes prevents a defensible damage assessment. A higher-quality post-event capture or additional sensor data is required to evaluate this candidate.

vlm.change_evidence: In the before image, a clearly defined elongated building with a green/vegetated roof is visible within the OSM footprint. In the after image, the distinct building structure is no longer discernible within the same footprint area, replaced by a uniform brownish-grey surface texture that could indicate rubble, debris, or collapsed structure. No clear building roof or walls are visible post-event.
vlm.before_observation: Clear aerial view showing an elongated rectangular building with a greenish roof within the outlined footprint. Surrounding context includes vegetation, a road to the south, and other buildings to the north. Image is sharp and well-resolved.
vlm.after_observation: The same footprint area shows no discernible building structure. Surface appears as uniform brownish-grey texture. Image is notably blurry/low-resolution. White patches in upper portion suggest possible cloud cover or image artifacts. Surrounding context features (trees, roads) are not clearly visible, indicating broader image quality degradation.
vlm.image_alignment: The yellow OSM footprint and red/white centroid reticle are positioned consistently in both images at approximately the same location, suggesting reasonable spatial alignment. The footprint shape and orientation match between scenes.
vlm.image_quality: Before image: high resolution, sharp, good visibility. After image: significantly lower resolution, blurry, possible partial cloud cover (white patches), reduced feature visibility throughout the scene. The quality differential limits confident damage interpretation.
vlm.uncertainty_reason: The after image has substantially lower resolution and appears blurry with possible cloud artifacts, making it difficult to definitively confirm building destruction versus just poor image capture. While the absence of a visible building structure in the after image is notable, the degraded post-event image quality prevents high-confidence damage classification. A human reviewer with access to higher-resolution post-event imagery would be needed to confirm major damage versus minor visible damage or no change.

Loss of shadow/height signature where a multi-story building previously cast a clear shadow.
vlm.before_observation: OSM footprint encloses a multi-story building with a clearly visible reddish-orange roof surface, defined edges, and cast shadow. Surrounding context shows trees, adjacent buildings, and a road network. Image is sharp with good color contrast.
vlm.after_observation: The same footprint area shows a pale, roughly uniform patch without visible roof lines, walls, or building edges. No cast shadow consistent with a multi-story structure is visible. Surrounding area appears washed out/hazy with reduced contrast. Lower spatial resolution and poorer color fidelity than the before scene.
vlm.image_alignment: Roughly co-registered. Yellow OSM footprint and red/white reticle sit over comparable ground features in both scenes; minor scale/perspective differences but the footprint itself is well-matched to the candidate building in the before image and to the altered area in the after image.
vlm.image_quality: Before: high resolution, sharp, good contrast. After: noticeably lower resolution, hazy/washed out, reduced color saturation, partly desaturated/pale tones, limiting fine detail interpretation. After image is usable but not ideal for confirming complete destruction.
vlm.uncertainty_reason: After image quality is materially lower (hazier, lower resolution, more washed out) than the before scene, which makes it difficult to distinguish between total destruction, severe collapse, or heavy debris coverage. The clear loss of roof/height is strong evidence of major damage, but the reduced fidelity prevents a high-confidence likely_destroyed call.

vlm.after_observation: The footprint area contains a dark, textured mass with scattered light pixels consistent with rubble. No coherent roof plane, parapet line, or building edges are visible inside the outline. Adjacent buildings are barely discernible due to heavy pixelation.

vlm.after_observation: No roof or wall structure visible within the footprint. The area shows a darker, irregularly textured patch consistent with rubble. Adjacent buildings to the right and bottom remain visible as lighter roof forms, indicating the loss is localized to the candidate.

vlm.uncertainty_reason: Lower resolution and pixelation of the post-event chip make it difficult to confirm whether the structure fully collapsed versus being reduced to a low rubble pile.
